title: WhatsApp → Safety Observation
subtitle: Demo 2 · site message, integrated to Forma

inputs.label: WhatsApp message
inputs.description: A short, unstructured hazard note sent from site through a messaging channel.
inputs.inputFormat: A free-text chat message, optionally with a site photo attached.

persona.quote: I fire off a quick message from site — someone in the office still has to turn it into a proper safety record.

stations.label: CAPTURE
stations.description: Pulls the message from its channel and confirms project context.

agents.name: Review
agents.description: Captures the message and confirms project context.
agents.output: A verified message tied to the right project.

stations.label: UNDERSTAND
stations.description: Interprets the note and classifies the hazard.

agents.name: Vision
agents.description: Reads any attached photo and recognises the hazard.

agents.name: Classification
agents.description: Extracts discipline, location and observation type.
agents.output: A tagged observation: type, location, severity.

stations.label: STRUCTURE
stations.description: Turns the understood note into a structured observation.

stations.label: ASSURE
stations.description: Runs the final checks before it is written to Forma.

agents.name: Data QA
agents.output: A completeness pass with any gaps flagged.

agents.name: Compliance
agents.output: A CONFORMING observation, ready for Forma.

outputs.label: Safety Observation
outputs.description: A classified, described safety observation created in Autodesk Forma.

steps.caption: A site foreman sends a quick, unstructured hazard message.
The message arrives with a short note and no structure.
The Review Agent captures the message and confirms project context.
The Vision and Classification Agents recognise the hazard and tag it.
The Ticketing and Naming Agents build a structured, referenced observation.
The Compliance Agent confirms the observation is complete and conforming.
A governed safety observation is created in Forma — from a casual message.
